metadata.name: Corrupted ETB condition fires when opponent has 3+ poison counters
metadata.description: Tests CR 207.2c (Corrupted ability word): Vivisection Evangelist has an ETB triggered ability with intervening-if Condition::OpponentHasPoisonCounters(3). P2 starts with 3 poison counters. When Vivisection Evangelist enters the battlefield, fire_when_enters_triggered_effects checks the condition, finds that P2 has 3 poison counters (>= 3), and proceeds to execute the DestroyPermanent effect. Due to a known DSL gap (AbilityDefinition::Triggered has no targets field), the DeclaredTarget { index: 0 } resolves to an empty target set and the destroy no-ops silently. The script validates that (1) the card ETBs correctly, (2) the Corrupted condition check executes without panicking, and (3) the game state is consistent afterward. The positive path (condition met, trigger fires) is demonstrated here.
metadata.generation_notes: Corrupted is an ability word (CR 207.2c) that marks intervening-if conditions of the form 'if an opponent has N or more poison counters.' In the engine, this is Condition::OpponentHasPoisonCounters(3) on AbilityDefinition::Triggered. The condition is evaluated in fire_when_enters_triggered_effects (replacement.rs ~L950) before executing the effect. With P2 having 3 poison counters, the condition evaluates to true and the trigger effect runs. The effect is Effect::DestroyPermanent { target: DeclaredTarget { index: 0 } }. Because AbilityDefinition::Triggered has no targets vec in the DSL, the EffectContext is built with an empty targets vec. The resolve_targets helper returns an empty vec for DeclaredTarget { index: 0 } when ctx.targets is empty (effects/mod.rs line 2444: None => vec![]). DestroyPermanent with no resolved targets is a no-op -- Llanowar Elves survives. This documents both the working condition check and the known targeting DSL gap. Vivisection Evangelist mana cost: {3WB} = 3 generic + 1 white + 1 black. P2 starts with 3 poison counters in initial_state to satisfy the Corrupted condition. The inline ETB path fires immediately on resolution (not via the stack), so no trigger_placed action is needed. Dispute documents the targeting gap per the card def TODO comment.

disputes.description: DSL gap: AbilityDefinition::Triggered has no targets field, so the Corrupted destroy effect (Effect::DestroyPermanent { DeclaredTarget { index: 0 } }) resolves with an empty target list and silently no-ops. The Corrupted condition check (OpponentHasPoisonCounters(3)) fires correctly per CR 207.2c and CR 603.4, but the full oracle effect (destroy target creature or planeswalker an opponent controls) cannot execute until the DSL supports declared targets on triggered abilities. See card def comment in vivisection_evangelist.rs: 'TODO: targets field on Triggered is not currently supported in AbilityDefinition::Triggered.' Llanowar Elves survives in this script as a result.

script.step_note: p1 casts Vivisection Evangelist; Corrupted ETB condition is checked against P2's 3 poison counters.

actions.description: Initial: Vivisection Evangelist in p1 hand; P2 has 3 poison counters (Corrupted condition satisfied); Llanowar Elves on P2's battlefield.
actions.note: P2's 3 poison counters satisfy the Corrupted intervening-if condition (CR 207.2c).
CR 601.2: p1 casts Vivisection Evangelist ({3WB}); 5 mana from pool. No spell-level targets needed.

actions.description: After casting: Vivisection Evangelist on stack, hand empty.
actions.note: Vivisection Evangelist on the stack awaiting resolution.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — stack resolves top object.

resolution.note: CR 608.2: Vivisection Evangelist resolves and enters the battlefield. fire_when_enters_triggered_effects checks Condition::OpponentHasPoisonCounters(3): P2 has 3 poison counters, condition is true (CR 603.4). Effect::DestroyPermanent { DeclaredTarget { index: 0 } } executes with no declared targets (DSL gap) — no-op.

actions.note: CR 603.4: Corrupted intervening-if condition is checked at trigger time. P2 has 3 poison >= 3, so the condition is met and the inline ETB trigger fires. The destroy target no-ops due to missing declared targets (see disputes).

actions.description: Final: Vivisection Evangelist on P1's battlefield; Llanowar Elves still on P2's battlefield (destroy no-op'd due to DSL gap); stack empty; P2 still has 3 poison counters.
actions.note: CR 207.2c: Corrupted condition was checked and satisfied (P2 had 3+ poison counters), confirming the intervening-if guard works correctly. The destroy effect no-op'd due to the known DSL gap. Llanowar Elves survives.
